steps.title: Find the common factor in both terms
steps.content:
Looking at $10x^2 + 130$, I identify what divides both terms evenly.

The first term is $10x^2$ and the second term is \$130$. I can rewrite each:
  • $10x^2 = 10 \cdot x^2$
  • $130 = 10 \cdot 13$
Both terms are divisible by 10.

steps.title: Factor out the GCF and identify the equivalent expression
steps.content: Factoring out 10 from both terms:$$10x^2 + 130 = 10(x^2 + 13)$$That matches choice B — B is correct.
